IRAN DEAL CLOSE: WAR PAUSE & NUCLEAR TALKS

The White House is nearing a one-page MOU with Iran to halt fighting and start nuclear talks. Iran is expected to respond within 48 hours; no deal is final.

Core terms:

Iran: Pause enrichment, no nukes, accept UN inspections, curb underground sites.
U.S.: Ease sanctions, release frozen assets.
Both: Loosen Strait of Hormuz restrictions.

The plan would trigger a 30-day negotiation window (likely Geneva or Islamabad). Trump has paused escalation; officials caution talks remain uncertain.

kept.text:
$PLTR Pentagon AI Shake-Up: Palantir Joins Massive 'Right to Integrate' Defense Overhaul
Palantir Powers Military Tech Revolution as Army Demands Open AI Battlefield Systems 🇺🇸🔮

05/06/2026
Summary:
The U.S. Army is teaming with Palantir and major defense firms to modernize military systems using AI and open integration 🪖

U.S. Army leaders are working with major defense and technology firms, including Palantir Technologies to improve how military systems connect and share data. The effort aims to bring artificial intelligence into existing platforms and streamline communication across systems. @PalantirTech 🔥

The initiative, referred to as the “Right to Integrate Hackathon,” is designed to address long-standing challenges where weapons and software systems operate separately. Palantir Technologies is among the firms expected to contribute to building a more flexible and connected architecture. 🦾

The first phase is expected to focus on air defense, drones, and missile systems, with participation from more than 50 platforms. Defense contractors such as Lockheed Martin LMT and Northrop Grumman are involved, alongside tech-focused companies working on AI-driven tools to support decision-making. 🤖

This initiative is part of a wider modernization of the process of defence procurement and increased development cycles. Officials hope that by encouraging open systems, which can integrate more effortlessly, the level of coordination will rise and the process of deploying its forces will result in faster outcomes and human control of the critical operations. 💯
By: Nauman Khan
🇺🇸🪖🦾🌟🔮💥

kept.text:
On this day in 2003 Palantir $PLTR was first founded

The company was founded by Peter Thiel, Alex Karp, Joe Lonsdale, Stephen Cohen, and Nathan Gettings in Palo Alto, California

Palantir was founded in 2003 in the aftermath of 9/11

Peter Thiel said the idea for Palantir came from observing two things happening at the same time: 

- The government had massive amounts of data but couldn't connect the dots to prevent terrorist attacks
- Silicon Valley had built sophisticated fraud-detection software for the financial system

Today Palantir is one of the top 50 largest public companies in the world worth more than $300 Billion

kept.text:
$PLTR Partner $SRFM Surf Air Mobility Announces Details of SurfOS Commercial Launch Plan

May 6, 2026
BrokerOS Commercially Live Since December 2025 and Growing in 2026.

OperatorOS Scheduled for Commercial Launch in Second Half of 2026.

SurfOS™ Enterprise Solutions Pipeline Is Active, Supported by Exclusive Palantir Teaming Agreement.

SurfOS Is Deploying AI Agents at an Accelerated Pace with Palantir’s Foundry and AIP.

LOS ANGELES--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Surf Air Mobility Inc. (NYSE: SRFM) (“Surf Air Mobility”, or the “Company”) today provided details on the commercial go-to-market strategy for SurfOS, the Company’s AI-enabled software operating system powered by Palantir Technologies (“Palantir”). SurfOS’s products serve the fragmented Part 135 private aviation and air mobility market through BrokerOS, OperatorOS, and SurfOS Enterprise Solutions, each monetized through distinct revenue models for brokers, operators, and large enterprise clients.

SurfOS targets an estimated $156 billion global market opportunity across three segments: chartered air transport, private aircraft sales, and MRO aftermarket. SurfOS is positioned to capture value across these interdependent markets by creating a connected ecosystem where supply and demand are optimized by leveraging Palantir's Foundry and AIP. 🦾🔮

Unlike pure-play software providers, Surf Air Mobility develops and validates every SurfOS product inside its own airline and charter businesses, including Southern Airways, Mokulele Airlines, and Surf On Demand private charter, before offering it to external customers. This operational proof-of-concept differentiates SurfOS because the software has been developed on real operational and commercial performance data.

Liam Fayed, Co-Founder of Surf Air Mobility, said: "We are one of the only companies in the aviation industry that both builds the software and runs the operations. We have the data infrastructure in place on Palantir’s Foundry platform and are developing agentic workflows and automation faster than ever before. The results are evident from the improvements to our business and financial performance. This track record is what will allow us to successfully sell SurfOS based on measurable outcomes.”

With the Company's federated operational data live within Palantir's Foundry platform, SurfOS is developing a suite of AI agents designed to autonomously optimize critical workflows, such as dynamic pricing, crew scheduling, aircraft sourcing, and AOG recovery. With Palantir's AIP providing the underlying AI infrastructure, the Company expects to deploy these SurfOS agents at an accelerated pace.

SurfOS Go-to-Market Overview:

BrokerOS

Go-to-Market: Commercially Live Since December 2025BrokerOS launched commercially in December 2025 through the Powered by Surf On Demand program that equips independent brokers with a branded platform offering a full suite of tools and an end-to-end solution, including 24/7 customer service, safety accreditation, real-time aircraft access, and complimentary aircraft recovery

BrokerOS deployed within Surf On Demand’s internal sales team delivered a 32% increase in bookings for top-performing brokers, 57% faster quote-to-close cycles, and 40% more payments processed on-platform comparing Q1 2026 vs. Q1 2025

Revenue Model:
BrokerOS generates revenue via a take rate across On Demand private charter bookings

2026 Commercial Target:100 independent brokers onboarded by year-end, contributing to Surf On Demand private charter business revenue and profitable margin

OperatorOS
Go-to-Market: Launching Second Half of 2026OperatorOS is designed for small and mid-sized Part 135 scheduled and charter air operators and is targeted for external commercial launch in the second half of 2026
Core modules within OperatorOS include crew and aircraft scheduling, reporting, and distribution, and will feed supply directly into BrokerOS
The Company currently has 17 Letters of Intent and software agreements in its initial sales pipeline generated from 440+ existing operator relationships

Revenue Model:
Modular subscription fee based on operator size, charged per module
Additional revenue generated from ancillary service upsells

2026 Commercial Target:10 additional letters of intent signed by year-end
Five operators onboarded and live on OperatorOS by year-end

SurfOS Enterprise Solution

Go-to-Market: Active PipelineSurfOS Enterprise Solutions provides fully customized deployments of SurfOS powered by Palantir’s Foundry and AI platforms for large operators, charter brokerages, and aircraft manufacturers
Palantir's forward-deployed engineering team participates directly in enterprise sales conversations under the Company’s exclusive teaming agreement

Revenue Model:
Customized agreements

2026 Commercial Target:
Targeting first multi-year, multi-million-dollar enterprise contracts in 2026
🇺🇸🦾✈️🔮🌟
https://t.co/wi0UWnOUWD

🦄 Jednorożec (70%+) – absolutni liderzy efektywności
$APP, $PLTR, $RBRK.

🦪Perły (50%-69%) – solidny balans wzrostu i marży
$DUOL, $FIG, $SNOW, $NOW, $ZS, $ADSK, $ZETA, $GTLB, $CRWD, $GRND, $DDOG, $S.

💎 Diamenty (40%+) – spełniające złoty standard SaaS
$ADBE, $CRM, $NET, $MNDY, $GRAB, $IOT, $TEAM, $DOCU, $WDAY, $PANW, $FICO, $INTU, $PATH, $AXON, $MDB, $SHOP, $ESTC, $OKTA, $HUBS.

Metodologia
🔹 Przychody – dynamika wzrostu przychodu (r/r)
🔹 Marża FCF – Levered Free Cash Flow Margin (TTM)
🔹 Rule of 40 – suma wzrostu przychodów i marży FCF
🔹 PEG  – wycena relatywna: prognozowany C/Z dzielony przez oczekiwany 3-5 letni CAGR EPS.

Dane: Seeking Alpha.

Patrząc na kolumnę YTD i wskaźniki PEG – widzicie tu okazje do odbicia, czy to nie koniec przeceny? Trzymasz którąś spółkę z listy w swoim portfelu? 👛 👇

パラ友各位

$PLTR Q1 2026決算時に公開された資料です。
整理してまとめました。
お時間ある方はご一読下さい🐕
$PLTR #palantir #パランティア #米国株

「Rule of 40」で見る異常値

$PLTR がQ1 2026決算時に示したこの資料は、同社の成長率と収益性を「Rule of 40」という指標で比較したものです。
対象は、時価総額上位100社規模の大型企業です。

この資料が示してるのは、 $PLTR が単に高成長企業であるだけでなく、「高成長」と「高収益性」を同時に実現している極めて例外的な企業として位置づけられている、という点です。

ーRule of 40とは何かー
Rule of 40とは、主にソフトウェア企業や成長企業を評価する際に使われる指標です。

計算式はシンプルです。

『売上成長率 ＋ 営業利益率』

この合計が40%を超えていれば、成長と収益性のバランスが良い企業と見なされます。

たとえば、売上成長率が30%、営業利益率が10%ならRule of 40は40%です。

一方、売上成長率が80%、営業利益率が60%ならRule of 40は140%となります。

ー $PLTR の数値ー
資料では $PLTR のRule of 40スコアは145%と示されています。

これは、直近四半期の売上成長率と調整後営業利益率を合算したものです。

グラフ上では $PLTR は右上に位置しています。これは、売上成長率が高く、かつ営業利益率も高いことを意味します。

多くの企業は、成長率が高ければ利益率が低くなりやすく、利益率が高ければ成長率が鈍化しやすい傾向があります。そのため、右上に位置する企業は限られます。

比較対象としてのNVIDIA、Micron、SK hynix

資料上では $PLTR の近く、またはそれ以上の領域に NVIDIA、Micron、SK hynix などが表示されています。これらはいずれもAIインフラ投資の恩恵を強く受けている企業です。

NVIDIA はAI GPU、Micron と SK hynix はAI向けメモリ、特にHBMなどで重要な役割を持っています。

つまり、 $PLTR はこの資料上で、半導体・AIインフラ企業と同じ「高成長・高収益」グループに並べられています。

ーポイントー
この資料の重要性は、 $PLTR が単なるソフトウェア企業としてではなく、AIインフラ企業に近い成長プロファイルを示している点です。

通常、ソフトウェア企業は高い営業利益率を持ちやすい一方、売上成長率がここまで高くなることは限られます。一方、半導体企業はAI需要によって売上が急拡大することがありますが、設備投資や景気循環の影響も受けます。

$PLTR は、ソフトウェア企業としての高い利益率を維持しながら、AI需要による高成長を実現している点が特徴です。

株主にとっての意味
株主にとって、この資料は $PLTR のバリュエーションを考える上で重要です。

株価が高く評価されている企業は、通常、その評価に見合う成長率と利益率が求められます。この資料では $PLTR が少なくとも現時点では、その高い評価を正当化し得る水準の事業パフォーマンスを示している、というメッセージになっています。

ただし、注意点もあります。

Rule of 40はあくまで一時点の指標です。今後も145%という水準を維持できるかは別問題です。高成長が続くには、米国商用事業、政府向け事業、AIPの導入拡大が継続する必要があります。

ーまとめー
この資料が示している核心は $PLTR が「成長か利益か」の二択ではなく、両方を同時に実現しているという点です。

Rule of 40が145%という水準は、通常の優良ソフトウェア企業の範囲を大きく超えています。

そのため、この資料は $PLTR を「単なるAI関連銘柄」ではなく、「高収益なAI実装インフラ企業」として見るべきだ、という会社側の主張を示したものと言えます。

tweet.text:
Everyone thinks I'm crazy.

I was crazy to sell covered calls on $TSLA when we hit $470 with a strike at $520.  

I was crazy to let my $PLTR short puts get assigned, take ownership at $140, and flip them at $170...

When you share your ideas and trades publicly, the crowd gets loud real quick.  

I'll be honest... I do this sometimes just to see what the MOB is really thinking.

Last night I posted about $SNDK and $MU... and I learned a ton.

Let's discuss:

$PLTR just dropped a 145% Rule of 40 score and it’s pretty unbelievable😂

Most companies are stuck well below the Rule of 120 frontier.

$PLTR is that bright green circle up top, 85% revenue growth + 60% adjusted operating margins = 145%.

It’s out here near the Rule of 160 frontier with the AI chip monsters ( $NVDA, $MU, SK Hynix).

Hypergrowth and fat margins at this scale are extremely rare for software. The operating leverage is insane and the AIP commercial flywheel is clearly working at full throttle.

This is why I’m long $PLTR. 

Fundamentals this clean don’t come around often.
